thesis: Gold is in a structural bull market driven by record central bank buying, fiscal debasement concerns, and geopolitical fragmentation. Fed rate cuts in 2026 will reduce the opportunity cost of holding gold. J.P. Morgan targets $5,055 by Q4 2026.

drivers: Fed cuts aggressively; real rates turn negative

risks: Fed holds or hikes on reaccelerating inflation

base_case.assumptions: 2–3 Fed rate cuts in 2026; steady central bank demand; moderate geopolitical tension; GLD returns 10–15% from current levels.

catalysts.event: FOMC meeting — rate decision and dot plot
BRICS summit — watch for reserve currency developments
